food_subgroup: Mixed meat and vegetable filling — Chinese dumpling / bun filling

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for pork-and-corn dumpling filling. FOODON_03315595 (pork-based food product) is the closest defensible ancestor term. A more specific term for mixed meat-vegetable dumpling filling is not available in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00GJ (Pig meat products) is the closest defensible category. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for pork-and-corn dumpling filling as a composite prepared food.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for pork-and-corn dumpling filling. SNOMED CT coverage of composite Chinese filling preparations is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of pork and corn filling is approximately 100 g, equivalent to the filling content of 4–5 medium-sized dumplings or 2 medium-sized steamed buns.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Ground Pork, Raw (FDC ID 168351) and Sweet Corn, Yellow, Raw (FDC ID 169998); values combined and adjusted for seasoning additions
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for pork-and-corn dumpling filling as a composite dish. Values are derived by combining USDA SR Legacy data for ground pork (raw, ~70% lean) and sweet corn (yellow, raw) in an approximate 70:30 ratio by weight, with minor adjustments for soy sauce and sesame oil seasoning contributions to sodium and fat. Energy, protein, fat, and carbohydrate values are consistent with the input record and cross-validated against USDA component data. Saturated fat estimated from USDA ground pork fatty acid profile. Input record values retained where consistent with USDA component data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on mixed meat-and-vegetable filling with low starch content
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pork-and-corn dumpling filling. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate based on: (1) the low-GI profile of sweet corn (GI ~52 per University of Sydney GI Database for boiled sweet corn), moderated substantially by the high protein (11.8 g) and fat (15.5 g) content of ground pork, which are known to blunt postprandial glucose response; (2) the low net carbohydrate content (9.5 g per 100 g); and (3) published GI values for comparable mixed meat-vegetable preparations in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 100 g serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g) / 100 = (35 × 9.5) / 100 = 3.3. This is a low GL. Note: when consumed with wheat dumpling wrappers, the overall meal GI and GL will increase substantially.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (380 mg per 100 g) from soy sauce and seasoning, combined with saturated fat from ground pork
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of pork-and-corn filling due to its high sodium content (380 mg/100 g); if consumed, choose reduced-sodium soy sauce, limit portion size, and avoid pairing with other high-sodium condiments such as dipping sauces.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low net carbohydrate content (9.5 g/100 g) and low estimated GI (~35), moderated by high protein and fat from pork
type2_diabetes.guidance: The filling itself has a low glycemic load (~3.3 per 100 g serving) and is relatively suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, total carbohydrate intake must be monitored when the filling is consumed inside wheat-based dumpling wrappers or buns, which significantly increase the glycemic load of the meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~5.2 g/100 g) and sodium (380 mg/100 g) from ground pork and soy sauce seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume pork-and-corn filling in moderation; substituting leaner pork mince, reducing soy sauce quantity, and increasing the proportion of corn and vegetables can meaningfully lower the saturated fat and sodium density of the filling.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat from ground pork may contribute to pro-inflammatory dietary patterns
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit high-fat pork preparations; the corn and ginger components provide modest antioxidant benefit, but the overall saturated fat load of the filling is not optimal for an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pork and Corn Filling (猪肉玉米, Zhū Ròu Yù Mǐ) is a popular modern Chinese dumpling and bun filling that combines ground pork with sweet corn kernels in an approximate 70:30 ratio. It is typically seasoned with ginger, light soy sauce, sesame oil, and a small amount of sugar or oyster sauce to complement the natural sweetness of the corn. The filling is mixed raw and then cooked as part of a dumpling (Jiaozi) or steamed bun (Baozi) by boiling, steaming, or pan-frying. The texture is tender and juicy, with the corn providing characteristic bursts of sweetness and a pleasant textural contrast against the savory, yielding pork. Nutritionally, per 100 g the filling provides approximately 225 kcal, 11.8 g protein, 15.5 g fat (of which ~5.2 g is saturated), and 9.5 g carbohydrate with 1.2 g dietary fibre. Sodium is relatively high at 380 mg per 100 g due to soy sauce seasoning. The estimated glycemic index of the filling alone is low (~35), moderated by the high protein and fat content of the pork, though the overall meal GI increases when consumed with wheat-based wrappers. Clinically, the filling is moderate-to-high concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to sodium and saturated fat, but low concern for Type 2 Diabetes when portion-controlled and consumed without high-GI wrappers. This filling has gained widespread popularity across mainland China and in international Chinese restaurants for its approachable, mildly sweet, and kid-friendly flavor profile.

confidence_reason: No single approved source entry exists for pork-and-corn dumpling filling as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central component data for ground pork and sweet corn in an estimated mixing ratio, with seasoning adjustments. Core macros are consistent with the input record and cross-validated against USDA component data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for ground pork (raw, ~70% lean, FDC ID 168351) and sweet corn (yellow, raw, FDC ID 169998) in an approximate 70:30 ratio, with minor sodium and fat adjustments for soy sauce and sesame oil seasoning. No exact composite entry exists in any approved source for this filling. 2. Saturated fat (5.2 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (6.8 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.9 g), trans_fat_g (0.1 g), and cholesterol_mg (55 mg) are estimated from USDA ground pork fatty acid profile and are approximate. 3. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for the mixed raw filling as prepared is not directly reported in USDA FoodData Central for this composite preparation. 4. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for pork-and-corn filling exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. The input record cited China Agricultural University as a source; this organization is not on the approved source list for Project Veritas v2. All nutrient values have been re-anchored to USDA FoodData Central. 6. Duplicate portion entry in input record (two identical 100 g entries) has been collapsed to a single serving definition. 7. This record describes the filling only, not the complete dumpling or bun with wrapper. Nutritional values will differ substantially for the complete assembled dish. 8. Halal status is marked false as pork is a primary ingredient.
